test: Effective diversity vs headcount diversity in swarm dispatch (F-COL1 test criterion 1)
method: Parse 691 unique DOMEX lane IDs from SWARM-LANES.md + archive. Compute Shannon H, Simpson D, Gini, concentration ratios. Cross-reference with per-domain lesson Sharpe scores (n=664).

proposition_tests.P5_elite_subset: CONFIRMED — top 2.4% of domains produce 29% of lanes
proposition_tests.P6_mediocrity_selection: CONFIRMED — most-dispatched domain (META) is below-median quality (#31/60)
proposition_tests.quality_concentration_tradeoff: CONFIRMED — concentration buys volume (+0.08 Sharpe) not quality

verdict: F-COL1 test 1 CONFIRMED. Concentration dynamics reduce effective cognitive bandwidth by 63%. UCB1 exploit bias produces rich-get-richer power law. Diversity pressure mechanisms (epsilon-greedy, soul_boost) insufficient — Gini 0.656 despite attempts.
next: F-COL1 test 2: model threshold θ where competence-authority mismatch triggers degenerative spiral
F-COL1 test 3: compare equal-weight vs expert-weighted aggregation across 20+ decisions
Test: increase UCB1 c parameter and measure diversity response
